integrityCheck.title: Database Integrity Check
integrityCheck.restoreButtonTitle: Will first try to apply migrations, then a fallback method

restoreSteps.step1: Tries to apply all migrations to head (the correct way)
restoreSteps.step2: If that fails, uses a fallback method via SQLAlchemy metadata
restoreSteps.manualAlternative: Alternatively, you can manually apply migrations using the "To head" button in the migrations section above.

migrations.title: Alembic Migrations
migrations.notInitialized: Database not initialized. Click "To head" to apply all migrations.
migrations.latestDescription: The newest migration in the code
migrations.applyXNewMigrations: {{count}} migrations need to be applied. Click "To head" to update the database.
migrations.allApplied: All migrations applied. Database is up to date.
migrations.howToUseHead: "To head" — apply all unapplied migrations to the latest (recommended)
migrations.howToUseApply: "Apply" — apply a migration from the input field:
migrations.howToUseApplyHead: head — apply all to the latest
migrations.howToUseApplyPlusOne: +1 — apply the next single migration (if current is 1a99f4734fc4, the next one will be applied)
migrations.howToUseDowngradeBase: base — downgrade all migrations to the beginning
migrations.howToUseClickHint: Or click the up arrow next to a specific migration in the table
migrations.statusExplanationTitle: What do the migration statuses mean:
migrations.statusAppliedDesc: The migration is already applied to the database. Tables/columns have been created.
migrations.statusPendingDesc: The migration has not yet been applied. It needs to be applied to update the DB.
migrations.statusHeadDesc: The latest migration in the code. There are no other migrations after it.
migrations.currentLabelHint: Current — label indicates the current database revision (the last applied migration).

downloadPage.alertLoading: Build information is still loading. Please wait...
downloadPage.alertNotAvailable: Build is not yet available. It will be available for download soon!
downloadPage.headerSubtitle: The only version of OrcaSlicer with an integrated materials catalog
downloadPage.screenshotTabDesc: A new tab right in the main OrcaSlicer window — materials catalog, settings search, and preset sync without switching between applications.
downloadPage.screenshotCatalogDesc: Full-featured FilamentHub materials catalog right in the slicer — browse settings, filter by printers, and import presets instantly.
downloadPage.screenshotSyncDesc: Automatic sync of your presets between FilamentHub and OrcaSlicer — work on any device, settings are always up to date.
downloadPage.screenshotImportDesc: Found the right settings? Import a preset directly into OrcaSlicer with one click — no manual file copying or folder searching.
downloadPage.videoDesc: See how FilamentHub integration works in OrcaSlicer
downloadPage.benefitAllInOneDesc: No need to open a browser, search for settings, and manually copy files. Materials catalog, search, and import — right in OrcaSlicer.
downloadPage.benefitSyncDesc: Your presets sync between FilamentHub and OrcaSlicer automatically. Work on different computers — settings are always up to date.
downloadPage.benefitLibraryDesc: Access thousands of verified settings from the community and manufacturers. Filter by printer, material, and rating — find the perfect parameters.
downloadPage.benefitVerifiedDesc: Use settings with high ratings and success rates from other users. Save time on calibration — print with quality from the start.
downloadPage.benefitFullDesc: This is a full version of OrcaSlicer with all features. We only added FilamentHub integration — nothing was removed or changed in the slicer.
downloadPage.benefitFreeDesc: FilamentHub Edition is completely free and based on open source code. All changes are available in our repository — transparent and secure.
downloadPage.buildNotReady: {{platform}} build is not ready yet. Compilation requires the corresponding operating system.
downloadPage.buildFromSource: You can build the version yourself from source code or download from GitHub.
downloadPage.included1: Full version of OrcaSlicer with all features — nothing removed
downloadPage.included2: New "FilamentHub" tab in the main slicer window
downloadPage.included3: Built-in materials catalog with search and filters
downloadPage.included4: Automatic preset sync between FilamentHub and OrcaSlicer
downloadPage.included5: One-click settings import directly from the catalog
downloadPage.included6: Preset management from the slicer — create, edit, export
downloadPage.installWin2: Run the installer and follow the instructions
downloadPage.installWin3: After installation, open OrcaSlicer — the FilamentHub tab will be available in the main window
downloadPage.installWin4: Log in to your FilamentHub account via the FilamentHub tab
downloadPage.installMacNotice: macOS build is in development. Compilation requires macOS with Xcode.
downloadPage.installMacAfterRelease: After release, the instructions will be:
downloadPage.installMac2: Open the .dmg file and drag OrcaSlicer to the Applications folder
downloadPage.installMac3: On first launch, macOS may request permission — click "Open" in security settings
downloadPage.installMac4: Open OrcaSlicer — the FilamentHub tab will be available in the main window
downloadPage.installMac5: Log in to your FilamentHub account via the FilamentHub tab
downloadPage.installLinux4: Open OrcaSlicer — the FilamentHub tab will be available in the main window
downloadPage.installLinux5: Log in to your FilamentHub account via the FilamentHub tab
downloadPage.installLinuxNote: AppImage does not require installation — just download, make executable, and run. For system integration, you can use AppImageLauncher.
downloadPage.faq1Q: What's the difference between FilamentHub Edition and regular OrcaSlicer?
downloadPage.faq1A1_post: with an additional FilamentHub tab. All original slicer features are preserved — we only added convenient access to the materials catalog right in the interface.
downloadPage.faq1A2_text: No need to switch between browser and slicer, all settings are available in one window. Automatic preset sync saves time, and access to a library of verified settings helps you print with better quality from the start.
downloadPage.faq2Q: Do I need a FilamentHub account to use it?
downloadPage.faq2A_text: You can browse the materials catalog and import settings without registration. An account is only needed if you want to sync your presets between devices, save favorite materials, and share your settings with the community. Registration is free and takes less than a minute.
downloadPage.faq3Q: Can FilamentHub Edition be updated separately?
downloadPage.faq3A: Yes! FilamentHub Edition updates independently and includes all original OrcaSlicer updates plus integration improvements. We follow new OrcaSlicer versions and regularly release FilamentHub Edition updates. New version notifications appear right in the slicer.
downloadPage.faq4A1_text: FilamentHub Edition is based on OrcaSlicer's open source code. All our changes are available in an open repository — you can check every line of code.
downloadPage.faq4A2: We only add secure integration via WebView — no changes to the slicer core, no external dependencies, no questionable modifications. All communications with FilamentHub go through HTTPS, tokens are stored locally in encrypted form.
downloadPage.faq5Q: What if I already have regular OrcaSlicer installed?
downloadPage.faq5A: FilamentHub Edition can be installed alongside regular OrcaSlicer — they don't conflict. All your OrcaSlicer presets and settings will remain in place. FilamentHub Edition simply adds a tab with the materials catalog without affecting existing configuration. You can use both versions simultaneously!
downloadPage.faq6Q: Does it work on all platforms?
downloadPage.faq6A1_post: (Intel and Apple Silicon) build will come later.
downloadPage.faq6A2_text: Windows (installer + portable) and Linux (AppImage) are ready to use. macOS will come later (building requires the corresponding system). You can also build a version for your platform yourself from source code — instructions are in the repository.

help.title: How to use your profile
help.presetsDesc: Presets are complete print settings for a specific filament: nozzle and bed temperatures, speeds, retraction, cooling, pressure advance, and much more. Each preset is linked to a specific material from the catalog and can be synced with OrcaSlicer.
help.presetsStep1: Click "New preset" on the "Presets" tab — the creation form will open
help.presetsStep2: Select a filament from the catalog. If it's missing — create a new one via the "+" button (enter brand, name, type, color)
help.presetsStep3: Fill in the main parameters: nozzle temperature, bed temperature, print speed, retraction. For PLA typically: nozzle 200–215°C, bed 55–65°C
help.presetsStep4: For fine-tuning, expand "Detailed settings" — tabs mirror OrcaSlicer: speeds, cooling, pressure/PA, overhang, etc.
help.presetsNote: Presets can be edited, duplicated, and deleted. You can also save other users' presets from the catalog via the "To profile" button.
help.printersDesc: Manage your 3D printers. Each printer contains printer profiles (bed dimensions, kinematics, G-code) and print profiles (quality: 0.08–0.32 mm).
help.printersStep1: Click "Create printer profile" — specify the model, bed dimensions, kinematics type, nozzle diameters
help.printersStep2: Add print profiles (Draft 0.3, Quality 0.2, Fine 0.12, etc.) — these define layer height and other quality parameters
help.printersStep3: If your printer isn't in the list — click "Request printer" and an admin will add it to the database
help.printersNote: Printer profiles sync with OrcaSlicer just like filament presets.
help.calculatorDesc: A tool for calculating 3D printing costs. Accounts for all key expenses: material, electricity, printer depreciation, work time, and your markup.
help.calculatorStep1: Select a filament and enter model weight (in grams) — material cost will be calculated automatically
help.calculatorStep2: Set print time, electricity rate, and printer cost for depreciation calculation
help.calculatorStep3: Add a markup (%) — the calculator will show the final price for the customer
help.syncDesc: FilamentHub is integrated with a modified OrcaSlicer. All your presets and printer profiles automatically appear in the slicer — no need to manually transfer settings.
help.syncStep1: Go to "Settings" (gear icon) and enable the "OrcaSlicer Sync" toggle
help.syncStep2: On each preset, click the sync icon (🔄) to make it available in the slicer
help.syncStep3: Open OrcaSlicer (FilamentHub version) — your presets will already be in the materials list
help.syncNote: Sync works both ways: changes in OrcaSlicer will also reflect on the website.
help.brandDesc: If you represent a filament brand, you can get a verified manufacturer account. This grants access to create official presets on behalf of the brand, which will be marked as recommended.
help.brandStep1: Switch to "Company Profile" at the top of the page
help.brandStep2: Fill in your company information and submit a verification request
help.brandStep3: Once approved by a moderator, you'll be able to add filaments and presets on behalf of the brand
help.settingsDesc: In settings you can manage your account, sync, and display preferences:
help.settingsStep1: Change your username, email, password, and avatar
help.tip1: Save presets you like from other users via the "To profile" button in the catalog — they'll appear in your list
help.tip2: Enable sync on each preset (🔄) to make it available in OrcaSlicer
help.tip3: Use material type (PLA, PETG, ABS...) and brand filters in the catalog
help.tip4: Click on a preset card to view all settings in a modal — you can also download JSON from there
help.tip5: Use the "Dashboard" tab for a quick overview: recent presets, statistics, activity

brandProfile.verifyStep2: Provide proof documents or contact information confirming your right to represent the brand
brandProfile.verifyStep3: Administration will review the information and make a decision within 3-5 business days
brandProfile.verifyStep4: After approval, you will automatically get access to the manufacturer dashboard
brandProfile.clickToViewDetails: Click on the request to view details and add files
brandProfile.option1Description: If your brand is already registered in the FilamentHub catalog.
brandProfile.joinVerifiedBrandInfo: After submitting the request, an administrator will contact existing representatives of brand {{name}} to confirm your membership. Make sure they are expecting your request. No proof documents are required.
brandProfile.brandNotVerifiedWarning: Brand {{name}} is not verified. To join, you need to provide proof documents, same as when creating a new brand.
brandProfile.brandNoEmployeesWarning: Brand {{name}} has no employees yet. To join, you need to provide proof documents, same as when creating a new brand.
brandProfile.option2Description: If you represent a brand that is not yet on the platform. After moderation, you will get access to the manufacturer dashboard.
brandProfile.checkBrandNotExists: Please make sure your brand is not already registered on the platform before submitting a request.

badgeDesc.founder: Project founder. Awarded to first users who joined FilamentHub at its early development stage.
badgeDesc.beta_tester: Beta tester. User who actively tests new features and helps improve the platform by reporting bugs and suggesting improvements.
badgeDesc.contributor: Contributor. User who made a significant contribution to the project: code, documentation, translations, design, or other useful materials.
badgeDesc.verified: Verified manufacturer. Official representative of a 3D printing materials brand who confirmed their affiliation with the company.
badgeDesc.early_adopter: Early adopter. User who started using FilamentHub in the first months after launch and actively participates in the community.
badgeDesc.supporter: Project supporter. User who financially or otherwise supported the development of FilamentHub.

adminMaintenance.title: Maintenance Mode
adminMaintenance.confirmEnable: Enable maintenance mode? The site will become unavailable to all users (except admins).
adminMaintenance.confirmDisable: Disable maintenance mode? The site will become available to all users again.
adminMaintenance.siteUnavailable: The site is temporarily unavailable for all users. Access to the admin panel and API for mode management is preserved.
adminMaintenance.siteAvailable: The site is available for all users.
adminMaintenance.messagePlaceholder: Example: Maintenance in progress. Estimated recovery time: 1 hour.
adminMaintenance.messageHint: If not specified, a default message will be used
adminMaintenance.info1: When enabled, all API requests (except /health and /api/v1/admin/maintenance) return error 503
adminMaintenance.info2: Frontend should handle 503 error and show maintenance message
adminMaintenance.info3: Administrators can log in and manage the mode through the admin panel
adminMaintenance.info4: Mode is stored in server memory (resets on restart)

adminBrands.slugInvalid: Slug can only contain Latin letters, numbers, hyphens and underscores

seo.defaultDescription: Filament catalog, printer profiles and print settings for 3D printers

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.subtitle: Report bugs, suggest features, or ask a question
feedback.sendError: Failed to send message. Please try again later.
feedback.messageSent: Your message has been sent. We will review it shortly.

bug.label: Bug
bug.subjectPlaceholder: Example: "Error when creating a preset on the filament page"
bug.messagePlaceholder:
Describe the issue in detail:

1. What were you doing before the error occurred? (steps to reproduce)
2. What behavior did you expect?
3. What actually happened?
4. On which page/section did the error occur?
5. Are you using a browser on mobile or desktop?
6. Do you have screenshots or error messages?

The more detail you provide, the faster we can fix it!
bug.instructions: For bugs, it is important to describe the steps to reproduce and expected behavior

feature.label: Suggestion
feature.subjectPlaceholder: Example: "Add a filter by material rating"
feature.messagePlaceholder:
Describe your suggestion:

1. What feature would you like to see?
2. How would it be useful?
3. How do you envision it?
4. Are there examples from other services that could serve as inspiration?

Your ideas help make FilamentHub better!
feature.instructions: Describe the feature, its purpose, and how it should work

question.label: Question
question.subjectPlaceholder: Example: "How to sync presets with OrcaSlicer?"
question.messagePlaceholder:
Ask your question:

Describe what you are interested in or what you need help with. We will try to answer as soon as possible!
question.instructions: Ask your question and we will definitely respond

other.label: Other
other.messagePlaceholder:
Describe your question, suggestion, or issue in detail.

Your feedback is very important to us!

wikiFeedback.title: Leave feedback
wikiFeedback.authMessage: Sign in to leave feedback on the article
wikiFeedback.placeholder: What did you like or what could be improved in this article? Your feedback will help make the content better.
wikiFeedback.sendError: Failed to send feedback. Please try again later.
wikiFeedback.feedbackSent: Your feedback has been sent and will help improve this article.
